question.label: Landing check-in
question.name: What’s been most on your mind at work lately, Riley?
question.description: Opens with a safe context-setting check-in so Riley can name what feels most salient in week two.

answer: I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
note: He sounds comfortable with the codebase itself but explicitly unsure how decisions get made, which is a mild clarity gap.

question.label: Decision-making map
question.name: When you say 'how decisions are really made here,' what feels most unclear?
question.description: Drills into the specific decision-making gap Riley just named so the manager can remove the right friction.

answer: I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
note: He can follow request paths but still lacks historical context behind tradeoffs, which is a mild remaining clarity gap.

question.label: Tradeoff context
question.name: Which tradeoffs feel hardest to read without the backstory?
question.description: Drills into the missing historical context he named so the manager can target the right orientation support.

answer: I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
note: He names a specific clarity gap: unclear domain ownership when priorities conflict; [THREAD-DEFERRED] domain ownership uncertainty should be picked up after advancing the arc.

question.label: Ownership edges
question.name: Where do the ownership boundaries still feel fuzzy to you?
question.description: Checks whether Riley knows where his remit starts and stops before he makes decisions.

answer: I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
note: He is withholding questions to avoid interrupting people, which strongly signals unclear norms for when and how to ask for context.

question.label: Notes doc questions
question.name: What kinds of questions are sitting in that notes doc right now?
question.description: Drills into the specific unanswered questions Riley is holding back so the manager can remove friction.

answer: I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
note: Names a specific missing guide on what to escalate versus batch, which is a strong clarity gap and signals hesitancy to engage in the moment.

question.label: Escalation guide
question.name: What would make the escalation line clearer in practice for you?
question.description: Surfaces the concrete cues or examples Riley needs to know when to escalate versus batch questions.

answer: The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
note: Names a concrete gap in live-incident handoff details, which is a strong clarity deficit and a mild sign of hesitancy to engage confidently in incidents. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'live incidents' practical handoff details worth drilling next session.

question.label: Missing context
question.name: What are you still waiting to understand before you change an architecture assumption?
question.description: Finds the specific context gaps that could lead to wrong technical decisions.

answer: A short weekly architecture review with someone senior would help me calibrate faster.
note: Names a missing support structure — regular senior architecture review — indicating a strong clarity gap and mild lack of growth calibration.[THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] Weekly architecture review with someone senior.

question.label: Support network
question.name: Who would you want to pull in first when you need context fast?
question.description: Looks for the people and channels that should become Riley’s default support network.

answer: If I know exactly what ownership I should take this month, I can contribute with more confidence.
note: Deficiency-as-request: he names missing ownership clarity as the condition for contributing confidently, signalling a strong growth support gap and mild clarity/engagement drag.

question.label: Next support
question.name: What would make the next couple of weeks steadier for you?
question.description: Closes by turning the check-in into concrete onboarding support and next-step alignment.

answer: I will ask one architecture question in the next incident review instead of keeping it in my notes doc.
note: He named a concrete next step that should reduce friction and increase clarity by asking an architecture question live instead of holding it back.
